headline: Israel's long obsession with southern Lebanon and the Litani River
description: Israel has intensified its deadly strikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon. The specter of a new invasion haunts the Lebanese population. The country's south, now a stronghold of the pro-Iranian Hezbollah, has preoccupied Israel and its founding fathers for over a century.

headline: France's military spending plans face reality of fragile national finances
Macron and Pope Leo XIV call for Middle East dialogue
Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
description: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

headline: US consumer inflation surges 3.3% year-on-year amid impact of Iran war
description: Gasoline prices surged by 21.2% between February and March – the largest monthly increase since the the government began publishing a gasoline price index in 1967.

headline: Ukraine gains upper hand in aerial war against Russia
description: Powered by a new generation of drones, Ukrainian forces carried out a record number of strikes in March. Russian oil and gas infrastructure became a primary target, impacting global energy prices.

headline: A French judge was found burned at a cliff in Djibouti in 1995. How and why he died remains a mystery
description: On October 19, 1995, the body of Bernard Borrel was found burned at the base of a cliff in Djibouti. More than 30 years later, his widow Elisabeth Borrel continues her fight to shed light on the case, which has become entangled in military interests, diplomatic pressure and state secrets.

AI is reshaping translators' work: 'Translation isn't simply converting words from one language to another'
description: New skills are needed, pricing pressure is mounting and 'post-editing' is on the rise. The rise of artificial intelligence is transforming the translation industry and training programs must adapt.

headline: World Press Photo 2026: Abdulmonam Eassa honored for his coverage of the war in Sudan for Le Monde
description: French-Syrian photojournalist Abdulmonam Eassa received the prestigious World Press Photo award on Thursday in the 'Stories' category for his photo report 'War in Sudan, A Nation Trapped,' carried out in October 2024 and December 2025 for Le Monde.

headline: Sarkozy sacrifices loyal ally at Libyan funding appeal trial
description: During his second day of questioning on Wednesday, the former French president was pushed to his limits by the presiding judge and did not hesitate to put the blame on his once close collaborator Claude Guéant, who was absent from the hearing.
